zh.Alnwick, Northumberland, England: 英國諾圖波藍德阿恩維克
zh.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross於1064年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾德內斯時，他的父親Malcolm III King of Scotland33歲，母親Ingebjørg Finnsdotter42歲。他於1086年與Euphemia de Balliol在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾德內斯結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1098年11月23日在英國諾圖波藍德享年34歲。

zh-hans.Alnwick, Northumberland, England: 英国諾圖波藍德阿恩維克
zh-hans.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross于1064年出生在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾德內斯时，他的父亲Malcolm III King of Scotland33岁，母亲Ingebjørg Finnsdotter42岁。他于1086年与Euphemia de Balliol在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾德內斯结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1098年11月23日在英国諾圖波藍德享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Malcolm,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: from the Gaelic personal name Maol-Choluim ‘devotee of (Saint) Columba’.

Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolcholuim ‘descendant of the devotee of Saint Columba’ (Irish Colum Cille, literally ‘dove of the church’). In Ireland the personal name is often spelled Colm; in Scotland it has become Calum.

variants.name: Malcolmson
meanings: Scottish (Shetland): patronymic meaning ‘son of   Malcolm  ’.

variants.name: Malcom
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Malcolm  .

variants.name: Malcomson
meanings: Scottish: patronymic from   Malcolm  .

variants.name: Malcomb
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Malcolm  .

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Malcolm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the medieval Gaelic name Mael Coluim ‘devotee of St Columba’. Columba was a 6th-century monk of Irish origin who played a leading part in the conversion to Christianity of Scotland and northern England; see also Callum and Colm . He has always been one of the most popular saints in Scotland, but in the Middle Ages it was felt to be presumptuous to give the names of saints directly to children; instead their blessing was invoked by prefixing the name with mael ‘devotee of’ or gille ‘servant of’. Since the 1930s the given name has been used widely in the English-speaking world, not just by those with Scottish connections.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: When Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross was born in 1064, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Malcolm III King of Scotland, was 33 and his mother, Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, was 42. He married Euphemia de Balliol in 1086, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 23 November 1098, in Northumberland, England, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross est né en 1064, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Malcolm III King of Scotland, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Euphemia de Balliol en 1086, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 23 novembre 1098, à Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross 1064 in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Malcolm III King of Scotland 33 und seine Mutter Ingebjørg Finnsdotter 42. Er hat 1086 in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Euphemia de Balliol geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 23. November 1098 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Northumberland, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross è nato il nel 1064 nel Holderness, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Malcolm III King of Scotland, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, aveva 42 anni. Lui sposato Euphemia de Balliol nel 1086, nel Holderness, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 23 novembre 1098, nel Northumberland, England, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Rossが1064年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスで生まれた時，父Malcolm III King of Scotlandは33歳，母Ingebjørg Finnsdotterは42歳であった。1086年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスでEuphemia de Balliolと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1098年11月23日にイギリスノースウンバーランドで34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross(이)가 1064년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Malcolm III King of Scotland(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Ingebjørg Finnsdotter(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1086년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 Euphemia de Balliol(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1098년 11월 23일 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: Holderness, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross nasceu em 1064, em Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Malcolm III King of Scotland, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Euphemia de Balliol em 1086, em Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 23 de novembro de 1098, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross родился в 1064 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Malcolm III King of Scotland было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Euphemia de Balliol в 1086 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 23 ноября 1098 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 34 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Malcolm mac Malcolm, Earl of Ross nació en 1064, en Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Malcolm III King of Scotland, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Ingebjørg Finnsdotter, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Euphemia de Balliol en 1086, en Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 23 de noviembre de 1098, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años.
